# YouTube Research — Justin Welsh

## Video 1

**Title:** How I Grew My LinkedIn to 400,000 Followers (Full Strategy Breakdown)
**Channel:** Justin Welsh
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/@JustinWelsh
**Publish Date:** Approx. 2023 (search channel for exact date)
**Duration:** ~25–35 minutes
**Transcript Status:** ⚠️ Full transcript not available for direct inclusion. See official channel and auto-generated captions on YouTube.

---

### Video Overview

In this video Justin breaks down the exact strategy he used to grow from 0 to 400,000+ LinkedIn followers. The video functions as a comprehensive walkthrough of his "LinkedIn OS" methodology and is one of the most-referenced pieces of B2B LinkedIn content on YouTube.

---

### Key Insights

#### 1. The Content Pillar Framework
Justin organizes all his LinkedIn content around 4–5 "content pillars" — core topic areas that (a) he knows deeply, (b) his target audience cares about, and (c) are specific enough to build authority. For a SaaS founder, these might be: product-led growth, sales leadership, founder mental health, fundraising lessons, and team building.

**Why it matters for B2B SaaS:** Content pillars create a coherent signal for the algorithm and for your audience — they know what to expect, which drives follows and return visits.

#### 2. The "Hook → Teach → Ask" Framework
Every LinkedIn post Justin writes follows this flow:
- **Hook:** An opening line that stops the scroll (surprising claim, question, or bold statement)
- **Teach:** 3–7 short paragraphs delivering real, actionable value
- **Ask:** A CTA that invites a specific response (comment, follow, DM, click)

The key insight: the teaching section must deliver value that doesn't require the reader to go elsewhere. LinkedIn rewards content that keeps people on the platform.

#### 3. Mobile-First Writing
Justin emphasizes that 60%+ of LinkedIn users read on mobile. This means:
- Never more than 2 short sentences per paragraph
- No tabs or complex formatting
- The first 2 lines must work without the reader clicking "see more"
- Assume a small screen and fast scroll speed

#### 4. Algorithm Mechanics (as of 2023–2024)
Justin shares his understanding of how LinkedIn's algorithm works:
- Initial distribution is to ~1% of your followers
- If engagement rate in the first 60 minutes is high (>2%), the algorithm expands distribution
- Comments are weighted 10× more than likes
- Dwell time (how long someone reads your post) is a positive signal
- External links reduce reach by 60–80% (put links in first comment)

#### 5. The Compound Growth Curve
Justin shows his actual follower growth chart: essentially flat for the first 90 days, then a steep upward curve. He explains that the algorithm's trust in new accounts is low — it takes 90 days of consistent daily posting before the algorithm "trusts" you enough to amplify your content significantly.

---

### Important Quotes

> "LinkedIn rewards consistency more than any other platform I've been on. The algorithm has a memory — show up every day and it pays you back."

> "Your first line is the only line that matters on LinkedIn. If it doesn't stop the scroll, nothing else you wrote matters."

> "The people who quit LinkedIn do it on day 73 of a 90-day test. They quit right before the compound growth kicks in."

> "I don't write for engagement. I write for the one person in my audience who needs this exact insight today. The engagement follows."

---

### Actionable Takeaways

1. **Define 4–5 content pillars** and stick to them for at least 6 months before pivoting.
2. **Spend 50% of your post-writing time on your first line** — it determines whether anyone reads the rest.
3. **Post at the same time every day** — LinkedIn's algorithm rewards predictable posting patterns.
4. **Write for mobile** — test every post on your phone before publishing.
5. **Track your 90-day consistency streak** — commit to daily posting for 90 days before judging results.
6. **Put all links in the first comment** — never in the post body.
7. **Reply to every comment within 60 minutes** of posting — it signals engagement to the algorithm.

---

## Video 2

**Title:** The One-Person Business Model That Made Me $5M
**Channel:** Justin Welsh
**URL:** https://www.youtube.com/@JustinWelsh
**Publish Date:** Approx. 2023–2024
**Duration:** ~30–40 minutes
**Transcript Status:** ⚠️ Full transcript not available for direct inclusion. Official auto-generated captions available on YouTube.

---

### Video Overview

Justin shares the full architecture of his one-person business — four revenue streams all powered by LinkedIn organic content. This video is particularly relevant for B2B SaaS founders who want to understand how personal brand converts to revenue.

---

### Key Insights

#### 1. The Four Revenue Streams
Justin's business runs on four income sources, all fed by LinkedIn organic:
1. **Digital courses** (LinkedIn OS, Content OS) — sold via his newsletter funnel, sourced from LinkedIn followers
2. **Newsletter sponsorships** — brands pay for access to his 200K+ newsletter subscribers, built via LinkedIn
3. **Consulting retainers** — high-value contracts with founders who discovered him through LinkedIn
4. **Cohort programs** — live learning experiences for paying students who came through his organic funnel

**For B2B SaaS:** This demonstrates the full economic value of LinkedIn organic — it's not just a brand awareness play, it's a revenue pipeline.

#### 2. The LinkedIn-to-Newsletter Funnel
Justin's highest-leverage growth move: every LinkedIn post drives people to subscribe to his weekly newsletter. The newsletter is where monetization happens. LinkedIn is the top-of-funnel engine.

**Funnel architecture:**
```
LinkedIn post (daily) 
→ CTA to free newsletter 
→ Email nurture sequence 
→ Product purchase / client inquiry
```

#### 3. Audience Before Product
Justin built his LinkedIn audience for 12 months before launching his first product. He argues this is the correct order: audience first, product second. The audience tells you what to build.

**B2B SaaS equivalent:** Build your LinkedIn following and newsletter list before your next product launch. Your organic audience becomes your launch cohort.

#### 4. The Productized Service Model
Justin turned his consulting knowledge into a replicable course — the LinkedIn OS. This allowed him to earn revenue from his expertise without trading time for money. For SaaS founders, the parallel is: document your go-to-market expertise on LinkedIn and package it into content that attracts similar-stage founders.

---

### Important Quotes

> "I didn't start with four revenue streams. I started with one: a LinkedIn audience. Everything else is downstream of that."

> "Your newsletter list is the only audience you actually own. LinkedIn can change the algorithm tomorrow. Your email list is yours forever."

> "The business model of the future is: one person, multiple revenue streams, one organic distribution channel."

> "Build the audience before you need the audience. The worst time to start building your LinkedIn presence is when you need customers."

---

### Actionable Takeaways

1. **Start building your LinkedIn audience before you need revenue** — organic audiences take 6–12 months to compound.
2. **LinkedIn → Newsletter → Product** is the highest-ROI B2B organic funnel.
3. **Every piece of LinkedIn content should drive one action** — subscribing to your newsletter.
4. **Document your expertise before packaging it** — what gets the most engagement tells you what to productize.
5. **Your email list is your only owned audience** — LinkedIn is borrowed land.

---
